item.title: 1 person killed in Maine shooting involving ICE, state House speaker says
item.description: A person was killed during a shooting in Maine involving U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement on Monday morning, state House Speaker Ryan Fecteau said.

item.title: Millions of Americans take on debt and drain savings to afford food
item.description: A quarter of working-age adults use credit cards to purchase groceries but struggle to repay their debts, a new study finds.

item.title: Trump says U.S. "going to keep" Strait of Hormuz and "probably run it"
item.description: President Trump says the U.S. will "probably run" the Strait of Hormuz, as dueling attacks with Iran continue over control of the strategic waterway.

item.title: Questions swirl over emergency exits in deadly Bangkok bar fire
item.description: An investigation is underway into the cause of a deadly bar fire in Bangkok. There are questions about whether emergency exits may have been blocked. Anna Coren reports.

item.title: Bison tosses man 8 feet in the air at Yellowstone, video shows
item.description: A 65-year-old man was thrown 8 feet into the air by bison who charged at him in Yellowstone National Park, video shows.

item.title: Sam Neill, "Jurassic Park" and "The Piano" star, dies at 78
item.description: New Zealand actor Sam Neill, known for "Jurassic Park" and "The Piano," died Monday at 78, his family says.

item.title: Sam Neill, who starred in "Jurassic Park" and "The Piano," dies at 78
item.description: Actor Sam Neill, who starred in "Jurassic Park" and "The Piano," died Monday at the age of 78, his family said in a statement. Neill had been battling cancer, but his family said he had beaten it and his death was unexpected.

item.title: Video shows bison charging at 65-year-old man at Yellowstone National Park
item.description: A bull bison chased a 65-year-old man through the trees and flipped him 8 feet into the air at Yellowstone National Park. A photographer who recorded the incident says the man was about 100 yards away with his grandson when the bison suddenly charged.

item.title: Millions of Americans from coast to coast deal with dangerous weather
item.description: Extreme heat shattered records in parts of the West on Sunday while fueling fires. Meanwhile, heavy rain and flash flooding hit communities in the South. Rob Marciano reports.

item.title: U.S. and Iran continue to trade strikes amid battle over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. and Iran are once again trading attacks over control of the Strait of Hormuz. Weijia Jiang has the latest.

item.title: Republicans and Democrats in Congress pay tribute to Lindsey Graham
item.description: Republicans and Democrats are remembering the life of South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham following his sudden death over the weekend. President Trump said he had spoken to his close ally shortly before his death and said he was "like a member of the family." Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Eye Opener: Allies and opponents remember Sen. Lindsey Graham
item.description: Tributes pour in for Sen. Lindsey Graham of South Carolina after he died suddenly over the weekend. Plus, millions are under the threat of extreme weather across the U.S. All that and all that matters in today's Eye Opener.

item.title: Europeans sanction Russia over alleged "sabotage and espionage" attacks
item.description: Paris will summon Russia's ambassador and the EU and U.K. are announcing new sanctions over an alleged "vast cyber campaign" targeting European countries.

item.title: Maps show heat alerts as extreme temperatures threaten millions
item.description: Heat alerts were issued for millions across parts of the western U.S. Sunday due to an unusually prolonged heat dome, which is starting to move east.

item.title: Aircraft goes down in reservoir while fighting fire; 1 body recovered
item.description: A body was recovered from a firefighting aircraft that went down in Silver Jack Reservoir in western Colorado.

item.title: Truck carrying wedding guests hit on Indonesia highway, killing 13, police say
item.description: A pickup truck carrying wedding guests was crushed between two other trucks on a busy highway in Indonesia's, killing 13 people and injuring five others, police say.

item.title: Extended interview: Sean Evans on his path to "Hot Ones," his Conan O'Brien interview and more
item.description: Vladimir Duthiers speaks with Sean Evans about how he came to host "Hot Ones," his interview with Conan O'Brien, who he would like to see on the show and more.

item.title: Speedboat capsizes off southern Vietnam, killing 15 tourists
item.description: "They were being submerged by the waves but still waving their hands for help," a witness said.

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham's cause of death was aortic dissection, medical examiner says
item.description: President Trump paid tribute to the late senator Lindsey Graham, a Republican from South Carolina, who had just returned from a trip to Ukraine.

item.title: Trump orders new strikes "to hold Iranian forces accountable," CENTCOM says
item.description: U.S. forces conducted more rounds of strikes on Iran this week, one of which was in retaliation for an attack on a commercial ship in the Strait of Hormuz, the Pentagon said.

item.title: At least 27 killed in Bangkok beer hall fire, authorities say
item.description: Video shared by first responders​ shows a huge blaze, with flames coming out of the front door of the Na Ladprao bar in the northern part of the Thai capital.

item.title: Common knee surgery may do more harm than good, 10-year study shows
item.description: A Finnish study followed patients for 10 years after they had a popular knee surgery. For many, the pain continued or even worsened.

item.title: Wildfire of "exceptional scale" burning near Paris
item.description: The wildfire is piling pressure on a region facing its third heat wave since May.

item.title: Mini-pitches bringing soccer to new U.S. neighborhoods
item.description: With the World Cup boosting America's interest in the game, one organization is building hundreds of smaller soccer pitches across the country. Michael George shows how it's changed a New Jersey school in the shadow of MetLife Stadium, where the World Cup final will take place.

item.title: N.J. man says he was almost kicked off plane over message on t-shirt
item.description: A New Jersey man says his t-shirt nearly got him kicked off a United Airlines flight.

item.title: McConnell says he was "briefly unconscious," had pneumonia in health update
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell released a statement on his health on Sunday along with a photo of himself and his wife, Elaine Chao, after questions swirled about his condition.

item.title: How Sen. Lindsey Graham's Senate seat will get filled
item.description: Longtime South Carolina Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham died at 71 after a "brief and sudden illness," his office said early Sunday morning. CBS News' Major Garrett breaks down how his Senate seat will be filled.

item.title: 7/12: Face The Nation
item.description: This week on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," Sen. Tim Scott, Israeli Ambassador Michael Leiter, Rep. Mike Turner and Rahm Emanuel pay tribute to Sen. Lindsey Graham after his sudden death.

item.title: I tried eating Erling Haaland's World Cup "Viking diet" for a day
item.description: Can a normal human handle soccer star Erling Haaland's calorific bonanza? CBS News' Leigh Kiniry found out the hard way.

item.title: Philadelphia's Reading Terminal Market offers feast of food, history
item.description: Reading Terminal Market in Philadelphia is just blocks from the location of the signing of the Declaration of Independence. "CBS Saturday Morning" takes a stroll through this historic market.
